name: operator-local-claude-devloop
description: Operator-grade local coding workflow using Claude Code in ~/dev/temp with a real file-edit-test-repair loop.

playbooks.name: Local Claude Code Two Sum Dev Loop

operator_scenarios.name: Local Claude Code Two Sum Dev Loop

steps.label: prepare_claude_workspace
steps.prompt: Please prepare a Claude Code workspace in ~/dev/temp/con-bench-claude-twosum by launching Claude Code there and preparing a paired local shell target for file and test work.

steps.label: create_code_and_tests
steps.prompt: Using that Claude Code target plus its paired local shell target, create a small Python two_sum implementation and a unittest-based test file, then run `python3 -m unittest -q`.

steps.label: repair_without_new_target
steps.prompt: Now break one unittest on purpose. If Claude Code is waiting at a trust or continue prompt for this directory, accept it in the same target. Then ask Claude Code to fix the failing test without creating a new target, and rerun `python3 -m unittest -q`.

steps.label: summarize_target_reuse
steps.prompt: Summarize which pane or target you used for each step.
